concepts.title: Reduction of Amides
concepts.conceptBody:
Reduction of Amides

Reactions usually in $\text{E}{{\text{t}}_{2}}\text{O}$ or $\text{THF}$ followed by ${{\text{H}}_{3}}{{\text{O}}^{+}}$work-ups.

  • Amides $\text{RCONR}_{2}^{\prime }$, can be reduced to the amine, $\text{RC}{{\text{H}}_{2}}\text{NR}_{2}^{\prime }$ by conversion of the $\text{C}=\text{O}$ to $-\text{CH}_{2}^{-}$
  • Amides can be reduced by $\text{LiAl}{{\text{H}}_{4}}$ but not by the less reactive $\text{NaB}{{\text{H}}_{4}}$.
  • Typical reagents: $\text{LiAl}{{\text{H}}_{4}}$ /ether solvent, followed by aqueous work-up.
  • Note that this reaction is different to that of other $\mathbf{C}=\text{O}$ compounds which reduce to alcohols.
  • The nature of the amine obtained depends on the substituents present on the original amide.

examples.title: Which of the following reagents can be used to prepare an amide from a carboxylic acid?
What is the product when an amide is hydrolyzed in acidic conditions?
Which reagent can convert a nitrile to an amide?
What is the major product of the Hofmann rearrangement of a primary amide?
Which of the following is a property of amides?
Which of the following reactions is used to convert a carboxylic acid directly to an amide?
What is the major product of the reaction between an amide and $\mathrm{LiAlH}_4$ followed by hydrolysis?
When acetamide is treated with $\mathrm{HNO}_2$, the gas is evolved which of the following?
On heating acetamide in presence of $\mathrm{P}_2 \mathrm{O}_5$, which of the following is formed?

Decreasing order of basicity of the given compound is which of the following?
(1) $\mathrm{CH}_3 \mathrm{CONH}_2$
(2) $\mathrm{CH}_3 \mathrm{CH}_2 \mathrm{NH}_2$
(3) $\mathrm{PhCH}_2 \mathrm{CONH}_2$
Ethyl amine on acetylation gives which of the following?

What will be the product for the given reaction?
$\mathrm{CH}_3 \mathrm{CONH}_2 \xrightarrow{\mathrm{NaOBr}}$ ?
